name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: Car title lender offering $100–$50,000 secured loans using your vehicle as collateral. Same-day funding, no credit check, bad credit and bankruptcies accepted since 2007.
description_long:
Montana Capital Car Title Loans is a California-based title loan agency that has operated since 2007 and claims to have helped more than 375,000 customers access emergency cash. The company markets itself under the registered trademark MontanaCapital® and positions itself as a fast, accessible lender for borrowers who cannot qualify for conventional financing. It holds a 4.5-star Google rating drawn from 59 verified customer reviews.

The core product is a secured car title loan where the borrower's vehicle title serves as collateral. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000, split into two tiers: a small-loan band of $100–$2,000 and a larger band of $2,000–$50,000. The company advertises a four-step process — apply online or by phone, receive a pre-approval based on income and vehicle equity, collect funds (via direct deposit or pickup at a local branch), and repay the loan while continuing to drive the vehicle. The title is returned once the final payment is made.

What distinguishes Montana Capital from competitors is its explicit acceptance of applicants with bad credit histories and active or prior bankruptcies, combined with a no-credit-check underwriting approach that it says has no impact on the applicant's credit score. Pre-approval is advertised at under five minutes. The service is accessible 24/7 through its website, and same-day funding is offered in footnote-qualified circumstances. Multiple reviewers cited same-day receipt of funds and friendly, responsive staff as standout positives.

Honestly assessed, Montana Capital fills a genuine gap for vehicle owners who have exhausted lower-cost borrowing options. However, the website discloses no interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules — a significant transparency gap for a product category known for very high borrowing costs. One customer review notes that loan proceeds are issued as a Bank of America check rather than cash, which may not suit all borrowers. The core risk of any title loan — vehicle repossession upon default — is not prominently communicated on the site.
address: 4538 S Pennsylvania Ave a, Oklahoma City, OK 73119

company_info.headquarters: 4538 S Pennsylvania Ave a, Oklahoma City, OK 73119

pros: Loan amounts up to $50,000 — unusually high ceiling for a title lender
Same-day funding available in qualifying circumstances
No credit check; bad credit and prior bankruptcies explicitly accepted
Borrower keeps driving their vehicle throughout the repayment period
Free pre-approval in under 5 minutes with no credit score impact
In operation since 2007 with 375,000+ customers served
4.5-star Google rating with multiple reviews citing fast turnaround and helpful staff
cons: No interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules disclosed anywhere on the website
Loan disbursement may be a Bank of America check rather than cash or instant deposit
Vehicle repossession is an implicit consequence of default on any title loan — not disclosed prominently
Only 59 Google reviews for a company claiming 375,000+ customers served raises questions about review coverage
State availability not listed — title loans are banned or heavily restricted in many US states
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor credit or prior bankruptcy who need fast emergency cash
Borrowers who need a larger secured loan ($5,000–$50,000) and cannot qualify elsewhere
People needing $100–$2,000 same-day with no credit check
Those who have been turned down by banks or personal loan lenders and own a car outright
diagnosis: Montana Capital is best suited for vehicle owners with damaged credit who need urgent access to cash and have no lower-cost alternatives — particularly those who need more than a typical payday loan can provide. The primary caveat is that the site discloses no pricing information whatsoever, and title loans as a product class carry high APRs and the risk of losing your vehicle if you default, making this a last-resort option that warrants careful comparison before committing.
services: Free pre-approval with no credit score impact
Bank of America check pickup at local branches
